Notice Of Motion & Motion For Sanctions For Disobedience Of Prior Court Order
Off Calendar Oct-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth Deeley for defendant and moving party: City and County of San Francisco
Melvin C. Belli for plaintiff and opposing party: Dorothy Mae Williams

No argument, tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. The request for monetary sanctions is granted. Failure to respond to discovery in a timely fashion and failure to appear for the independent medical examination was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1200 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. The motion for evidentiary sanctions is granted. Plaintiff is precluded from offering as evidence any material responsive to written discovery that was not provided to defendant by October 12, 2005. The request for terminating sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar May-12-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Plaintiff/opposing party Dorothy Mae Williams appearing in propria persona

RULING:
No argument was made. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED:
1) Defendant's motion to compel further responses to request for production set 1:
Defendant's motion is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, as amended by AB 3078, Chapter 171, Statutes of 2004, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed on April 15, 2005 and served by mail.

2) Defendant's motion to compel responses to special interrogatories set #2
Defendant's motion is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, as amended by AB 3078, Chapter 171, Statutes of 2004, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed on April 15, 2005 and served by mail. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
